summary: MongoDB::getCollectionInfo() should trim database prefix when querying system.namespaces
MongoDB::getCollectionInfo() should return a numerically indexed array
description:
Returning a numerically indexed array is consistent with {{MongoCollection::getIndexInfo()}}.

If users need to access a specific collection's metadata, using the {{filter}} option is more efficient than returning all collection metadata for the database and accessing a single key.

Since this function is new to 1.6.0, this change will not be a BC break.

MongoCollection::save() with options crashes
Unable to update field '0' using an array.
Driver throw errors after replica-set primary switch
description:
We've setup a replicaset, 1 primary, 1 secondary and 1 arbiter; 
Server is configured with ssl.

Everything works fine before we do db switch.

Here're the steps:
a. On primary db server, I execute rs.stepDown(5) to make secondary sever promoted as primary.
b. Refresh page (with read and write) right after the above command.
c. I got following errors randomly:

   1. MongoClient::__construct(): SSL: Connection reset by peer
   2. MongoClient::__construct(): SSL: Broken pipe
   3. MongoClient::__construct(): SSL operation failed with code 1. OpenSSL Error messages: error:1409F07F:SSL routines:SSL3_WRITE_PENDING:bad write retry
   4. MongoClient::__construct(): SSL operation failed with code 1. OpenSSL Error messages: error:140940F6:SSL routines:SSL3_READ_BYTES:unknown alert type in ..php on line 14

I can understand that #1 and #2 can happen right after switch because php was still hold the old primary server's connection and result in connection lost after switch. 

But someone how all these 4 errors keeps happening after the switch and make the application unstable, especially the #3, this is the most frequent error.

summary: Core dump if name resolution is wrong for MongoDB servers
description:
We have noticed core dumps from time to time by PHP processes running MongoDB connections. I Suspected a transient DNS problem, and I managed to replicate this problem by deliberately breaking /etc/hosts. It is possible if you pass the wrong IPs for a hostname in /etc/hosts to cause the PHP MongoDB driver to dump core, at least under FPM in 5.3.

I have a three node replica set, A and B are full nodes and mgm is a arbitrator. All works fine with this in /etc/hosts:

10.99.99.151    mongodb-a.domain.tld
10.99.99.152    mongodb-b.domain.tld
10.99.99.150    mongodb-mgm.domain.tld

If I change this to this (broken) config:

10.99.99.150    mongodb-a.domain.tld
10.99.99.151    mongodb-b.domain.tld
10.99.99.152    mongodb-mgm.domain.tld

Every request results in a core file (core attached).

I am connecting to MongoDB using IP addresses only (mongodb://10.99.99.151, 10.99.99.151) but the MongoDB replica set is build using FQDNs.

I'm pretty sure that this is a bug, as while my name resolution is broken it should result in a can't connect error (or similar), not core fine. I'm guessing this has something to do with following a referral from the first node that is correctly connected, but i'm not really sure.

Thanks!

summary: Support URL encoded characters in connection string
Handle private/protected properties with insert/update in zval_to_bson by supporting __sleep
description: See also: https://groups.google.com/d/topic/mongodb-user/-iX9MRmJP5A/discussion

summary: Break BC! Throw exceptions for ctor failure
description:
We need to fix our ctors to throw exceptions on failure.
Lot of applications actually do $m = new Mongo(..); if ($m) { ... } at the moment so we cannot do it in the v1.2 branch.

summary: Connection timeout on first write operation
description:
We tested it on different client/server configuration.

The first write operation on the mongodb server throw an exception:
"Failed to connect to: mongos:27017: Read timed out after reading 0 bytes, waited for 0.000000 seconds"

All subsequents operations are successful.

summary: New query options and response flags
description: Two new query options (AwaitData and Exhaust) and all new response flags (CursorNotFound, QueryFailure, and AwaitCapable) have been added to the Mongo wire protocol (http://www.mongodb.org/display/DOCS/Mongo+Wire+Protocol). The driver should handle the response flags appropriately (eg. CursorNotFound should throw exception rather than silently end the query results). The driver may implement the new query options if desired.

summary: Pin per-request connection to a secondary
description:
We should be pinning secondary connections to the objects, unless the Read Preference changes, for the lifcycle of an request.
Otherwise we risk reading from random secondaries per-query, resulting in possibly inconsistent data.

summary: Mongo-enabled PHP scripts became unresponsive after failure in replica set SECONDARY
description:
We encountered a very strange behavior with the PHP driver. One of the secondaries in our replica set became unhealthy. Soon (but not immediately!) all PHP scripts connecting the replica set became unresponsive. They did not fail, but apparently timed out. As soon as the unhealthy secondary was removed from the replica set, all PHP scripts started to work properly.

Some miscellaneous facts:
* The failed secondary was not in the initial server list in PHP
* Rebooting the PHP processes or MongoDB servers did not help
* Connecting any of the servers over mongo cli worked well (this leads me to think this problem is in the PHP driver)

summary: GridFS: Ability to set file _id for custom shard key usage
description: It looks like other drivers (Java, at least; see: http://groups.google.com/group/mongodb-user/msg/524bae1602770587) have implemented a way to set the GridFS _id for files. But it doesn't appear to be an option in the PHP driver. Would be nice to have if it's practical.

summary: The Mongo object has not been correctly initialized by its constructor
description:
A ->selectionCollection('drupal', 'simpletest509769config') call dies with  Uncaught PHP Exception MongoException: "The MongoClient object has not been correctly initialized by its constructor".

According to xdebug the MongoClient object connected is false and status, server, persistent are all null. The client has been created previously with connect true option and then kept alive as a property on the factory. But apparently it somehow got corrupted. Trying to run ->connect() on the object throws the same exception.

summary: PHP Group function stop working with MongoDB 2.4.3
description: The php group function stop working after upgrade to MongoDB 2.4.3. The function works perfect with MongoDB 2.4.1 and the same group function works fine trought the javascript console (2.4.3 and 2.4.1). I try php driver 1.3.6, 1.3.7 and 1.4RC and none works with MongoDB 2.4.3

summary: MongoGridFSFile.getResource should specify 'stream' instead of 'resource' as return type
description:
This is the current document block for the function:

{noformat}
/**
 * This method returns a stream resource that can be used to read the stored file with all file functions in PHP.
 * The contents of the file are pulled out of MongoDB on the fly, so that the whole file does not have to be loaded into memory first.
 * At most two GridFSFile chunks will be loaded in memory.
 *
 * @link http://php.net/manual/en/mongogridfsfile.getresource.php
 * @return stream Returns a resource that can be used to read the file with
 */
{noformat}

Since 'stream' isn't a recognized php type this creates problems in IDEs that use type inference for autocompletion / code validation.

It should read :

{noformat}
/**
 * This method returns a stream resource that can be used to read the stored file with all file functions in PHP.
 * The contents of the file are pulled out of MongoDB on the fly, so that the whole file does not have to be loaded into memory first.
 * At most two GridFSFile chunks will be loaded in memory.
 *
 * @link http://php.net/manual/en/mongogridfsfile.getresource.php
 * @return resource Returns a resource that can be used to read the file with
 */
{noformat}

summary: MongoCollection::hasNext() returns false for limit=1 result
Segmentation Fault Mongo PHP 5.5
description:
I'm seeing a segmentation fault when using findAndModify to modify a single nested subdocument.

Changing from findAndModify to an update query and a findOne query appears to resolve the issue.

Please let me know what is required to formalise the bug report.

summary: Default writeConcern not write safe on standalone node
MongoDB::command with 'distinct' and without 'query' doesn't work anymore
Hostname matching with Replica Set doesn't work pre PHP 5.6
description:
PHP 5.6.0+ enables hostname matching by default.
This is done by automatically extracting the peer name from the hostname we are creating a connection to.

In PHP before 5.6.0 the user had to provide PHP with the hostname as part of the SSL context.

This hostname is singular. Its only one hostname -- the user cannot provide multiple, like for an entire Repilca Set.
Besides, the user shouldn't have to provide this value -- we do auto discovery of nodes, which may change on any given time, so for the user to provide the full list doesn't make a lot of sense.

summary: Add certification expiration matching
Connecting through SSL no longer works in PHP 5.6 with 2.4.13, 2.6.6, and later
description:
PHP 5.6 (incorrectly) redefined the meaning of STREAM_CRYPTO_METHOD_SSLv23_CLIENT.
Using SSLv23 now explicitly removes TLS support, leaving only SSLv2 and SSLV3 available.

MongoDB has never supported SSLv2, but did support SSLv3 until 2.4.13/2.6.6 which explicitly disabled SSLv3.

This leaves no available protocols in PHP 5.6.0-5.6.6.
This has been reported as https://bugs.php.net/bug.php?id=69195

summary: Compile error when OpenSSL is not available
description:
Currently the master branch on Github is broken for my environment.
Steps to reproduce:

{code}
git clone https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver.git
cd mongo-php-driver/
phpize
./configure
make
{code}

Error output
{code}
/bin/bash /tmp/mongo-php-driver/libtool --mode=compile cc -I./util -I. -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver -DPHP_ATOM_INC -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/include -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/main -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver -I/usr/local/include/php -I/usr/local/include/php/main -I/usr/local/include/php/TSRM -I/usr/local/include/php/Zend -I/usr/local/include/php/ext -I/usr/local/include/php/ext/date/lib -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/api -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/util -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/exceptions -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/gridfs -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/types -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/batch -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/contrib -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/mcon -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/mcon/contrib  -DHAVE_CONFIG_H  -g -O2   -c /tmp/mongo-php-driver/io_stream.c -o io_stream.lo
 cc -I./util -I. -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver -DPHP_ATOM_INC -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/include -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/main -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver -I/usr/local/include/php -I/usr/local/include/php/main -I/usr/local/include/php/TSRM -I/usr/local/include/php/Zend -I/usr/local/include/php/ext -I/usr/local/include/php/ext/date/lib -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/api -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/util -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/exceptions -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/gridfs -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/types -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/batch -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/contrib -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/mcon -I/tmp/mongo-php-driver/mcon/contrib -DHAVE_CONFIG_H -g -O2 -c /tmp/mongo-php-driver/io_stream.c  -fPIC -DPIC -o .libs/io_stream.o
/tmp/mongo-php-driver/io_stream.c:67:57: error: unknown type name ‘X509’
make: *** [io_stream.lo] Error 1
{code}
